sections.paragraph: δρόμος, ου, ὁ [cp. the aor. inf. δραμεῖν of τρέχω; primarily of an activity, ‘running, race’, then the place for running, ‘course’] the idea of movement over a prescribed distance is extended to achievement of an assignment, course Ac 13:25; 20:24; in specific imagery of the games τὸν δ. τετέλεϰα I’ve finished the race 2 Ti 4:7.
